fields.helpText: Enter in this field the patient associated with this income relation.
Enter in this field the individual whose income is associated with this income relation.
Enter 'YES' in this field if the veteran's marital status was either married or separated on Dec 31st of last calendar year.  Otherwise, enter 'NO'.
Enter whether the veteran and relation lived together last calendar year.

This response must be a number.   If the veteran did not live with his or her spouse, enter in this field the amount the veteran contributed to his or her spouse last calendar year.   If the amount the veteran contributed is less than $600, then the spouse will not be considered a dependent for financial assessment.

Enter in this field whether the veteran had any UNMARRIED children or stepchildren under the age of 18 or between the ages of 18 and 23 and attending school, or any unmarried children over the age of 17 who became permanently incapable of self-support before reaching the age of 18 last calendar year.   School attendance does not have to be full-time but it should be regular.   The child can be enrolled in high school, college, or vocational school.   If the worksheet is being completed during a
Enter in this field whether the child is unmarried, over the age of 17, and became permanently incapable of self-support before reaching in age of 18.

Enter in this field a Yes or No to indicate whether the veteran contributed any dollar amount to the child's support last calendar year.  The contributions do not have to be in regular set amounts. For example, a veteran who paid a child's school tuition or medical bills would be contributing to the child's support.

This field is computed by the system.  Dependent children include any unmarried children or stepchildren who are under the age of 18 or between the ages of 18 and 23 and attending school, or any unmarried children over the age of 17 who became permanently incapable of self-support before reaching the age of 18.
Enter 'YES' in this field if the veteran's marital status currently is either married or separated.  Otherwise, enter 'NO'.
Enter whether the veteran and relation are living together.
Enter 'YES' if the spouse or dependent is residing in the community. Enter 'NO' if the spouse or dependent is institutionalized.
Enter 'YES' in this field if the veteran and spouse are legally separated.

Enter in this field the dollar amount that the veteran contributed to the child's support last calendar year.  The contributions do not  have to be in regular set amounts.  For example, a veteran who paid a child's school tuition or medical bills would be contributing to the child's support.

This response must be a 'YES' or 'NO'    If the veteran did not live with his or her spouse, enter in this field the veteran's statement as to whether he or she contributed to his or her spouse's support last calendar year.                                                                     If the veteran did not contribute to the spouse, then the spouse will not be considered a dependent for financial assessment.
